title: We Are Not Machines by Sarah O’Connor review – can dignity at work survive the tech revolution?
summary: A Financial Times journalist ponders the future of labour in world increasingly dominated by AI and automationIt’s never been easy to land and keep a decent job. But it feels like it’s getting harder. In June, the number of job vacancies in the UK fell to a five-year low; headlines warn of a looming AI-employment shock. What might the future of work look like – and who or what will shape its terms? In her new book, Sarah O’Connor goes looking for answers in the modern collision of artificial int
title_cn: 莎拉·奥康纳的《我们不是机器》评论——工作中的尊严能否在科技革命中幸存下来？
summary_cn: 英国《金融时报》记者思考在人工智能和自动化日益主导的世界中劳动力的未来找到并保持一份体面的工作从来都不是一件容易的事。但感觉越来越难了。
translation:
【标题】莎拉·奥康纳的《我们不是机器》评论——工作中的尊严能否在科技革命中幸存下来？
【摘要】英国《金融时报》记者思考在人工智能和自动化日益主导的世界中劳动力的未来找到并保持一份体面的工作从来都不是一件容易的事。但感觉越来越难了。

title: Reform would have received a fraction of £26.7m donations haul under a £100,000 cap, analysis shows

title: Martha Lillard, last known US polio survivor using iron lung, dies aged 78
summary: Lillard, of Oklahoma, contracted polio when she was five and slept inside cylindrical metal device to help her breatheThe last known US person living with polio and relying on an iron lung has died aged 78.Martha Lillard, who contracted polio at age five and spent most of her life dependent on an iron lung machine that helped her breathe, died on 26 June in Oklahoma, according to an online obituary.
